articles.title: AI AT WORK: WHAT YOUR TEAMS WISH YOU KNEW
articles.summary: Miro surveyed 2,000+ engineering, product, and design professionals on AI adoption. The surprising finding? While 75% are optimistic about AI's potential and twice as likely to feel excited rather than anxious [4], one-third aren't using AI tools at all—with product teams lagging furthest behind.

articles.title: with leadership support that makes experimentation feel safe
articles.summary: rather than risky. The report includes a three-part framework for moving teams from AI curiosity to confidence.

articles.title: THE AI APPLICATION SPENDING REPORT: WHERE STARTUP DOLLARS REALLY GO
articles.summary: AI-native startups are redefining software by integrating AI into every workflow, unlike big firms gaining only incremental gains. Emerging trends show horizontal dominance, rising “vibe coding,” and consumer-to-enterprise product evolution driving widespread AI adoption.

articles.title: SORA, AI BICYCLES, AND META DISRUPTION
articles.summary: Sora wins by making creation effortless for everyone, which could upend Instagram's entertainment feed, weaken Meta's attention model, and redefine who gets to be a creator.

articles.title: MALTE SCHOLTZ ON FIXING MISALIGNMENT, MASTERING AI FLUENCY, AND THE VALUE OF PRODUCT OPS
articles.summary: Product success depends on alignment built through trust and shared context, not control. As AI automates tasks, PMs must excel in data storytelling, influence, and Product Ops to scale clarity and impact.

articles.title: THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN ITERATION AND TRUE INNOVATION
articles.summary: Iteration refines what exists. Innovation redefines what's possible. True innovation challenges assumptions, embraces risk, and creates new value, while iteration sustains progress through steady improvement.

articles.title: COST-PLUS CREDITS MODELS ARE A QUICK FIX FOR AI MONETIZATION - NOT THE FINAL DESTINATION
articles.summary: In cost-plus credit models, customers prepay for credits, which are consumed when they run AI-powered tasks. They're popular with everyone from new startups to players like OpenAI - but they also create misalignment between pricing and customer value [9]. Read about the limitations of AI credits (and what comes next) on the Metronome blog.

articles.title: AI & COGNITIVE DEBT
articles.summary: AI enhances productivity but risks dulling our cognitive “create mode” by pushing us into passive “review mode.” To stay sharp, outsource knowledge to AI but protect reasoning - use it to learn, not to think for you.

articles.title: HIGHLIGHTS FROM THE 2025 IA SUMMIT
articles.summary: AI's future is shifting toward agent-first systems, smarter infrastructure, and new human-AI interfaces. Startups and incumbents alike are racing to embed reasoning, context, and adaptability into every workflow.

articles.title: THE DEATH OF SEARCH: HOW SHOPPING WILL WORK IN THE AGE OF AI (45 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: AI will own mid-funnel shopping by researching and auto-buying clear SKUs at the best price, pressuring search and affiliate economics, while impulse and big in-person purchases stay largely human. Expect trust-first curation and AI-native tools to reshape pricing, recommendations, and checkout.

articles.title: LET'S MAKE IT AWKWARD
articles.summary: Embracing discomfort can reclaim power and drive change. Speaking up, even when it feels awkward, challenges bias and redefines worth.

articles.title: SUCCESS IN SAAS
articles.summary: SaaS success demands excellence from distribution to renewal, not just product building.

articles.title: OPENAI APPS OPPORTUNITY
articles.summary: For consumer product builders, it's a one-month all-in bet - small chance it's the next App Store, huge upside if you're early.
